text.title: use-case-mapping Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for use-case-mapping. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Main flows must stay at user-goal level. Limit to 10 steps maximum. If longer, split into sub-use-cases. Write actor steps first, system response second to keep flow actor-centric.
rule.rationale: User-goal level keeps documentation readable and maintainable. Too much detail about screens and buttons locks the design too early. Actor-first perspective ensures the documentation serves users, not just systems.

rule.statement: Prioritize alternatives by production likelihood and user impact, not edge-case completeness. Aim for 3 to 5 alternatives per main flow, sorted by frequency of occurrence and severity of impact.
rule.rationale: Exhaustive enumeration leads to document bloat and analysis paralysis. Focus on what actually happens in production with real users. Estimate frequency from interviews, analytics, or support tickets if available. Leave theoretical edge cases for later or as notes.
